acceptance_thresholds.purpose: numerical qualification only; not experimental validation

rejected_forward_particle_tally.reason: direct hit counts were sparse over thousands of evolving triangles and did not converge monotonically
rejected_forward_particle_tally.lower_sample_outcome: physical-volume topology refusal caused by zero-count and overweight face velocities

long_duration_time_step_after_consistent.interpretation: The long-horizon absolute increment is not qualified at the 3% relative gate, although both changes remain below one 20 nm cell. A 200 s full-chemistry control differs by only 0.10%, so the discrepancy appears after deeper geometry evolution and is not a chemistry-only effect. Do not promote an absolute-rate claim from this ladder; test convergence of the duration-free width ratios directly.

long_duration_width_ratio_failure.interpretation: Width normalization does not cancel the long-horizon time-step error. The 80 nm trajectory crosses a numerical or model-state etch-stop bifurcation. Bracket its onset and inspect polymer/complex state before any physical calibration.

zero_seed_80nm_time_step_followup.interpretation: The 5 s trajectory numerically tunnels through an etch-stop state. The 2.5 and 1.25 s trajectories both approach a shallow polymer-limited branch, although their stop depths are not yet within 3%. Further time refinement of the unsupported chemistry closure is deferred in favor of mechanism ablation.

seed_free_followup.reason: CSG material ownership was corrected so a flat substrate can expose and initialize new sidewalls without an artificial pre-etched seed
seed_free_followup.conclusion: Zero-seed surface birth passes, but the 60 nm trench is not spatially qualified: 0.01 to 0.005 um changes the shallow increment by 6.70%. Use 0.005 um only as a provisional narrow-width level until finer, AMR, or symmetry-reduced evidence closes the 3% gate.

coarse_four_cells_across.outcome: false etch-stop branch with a one-cell visibility transition near 250-300 s

refined_eight_cells_across.outcome: smooth monotone depth histories; coarse-grid etch-stop bifurcation removed; 2.5-to-1.25 s refinement passes the 3% gate

eighty_nm_resolution_root.validity_rule: Do not qualify an evolving opening with fewer than eight cells across its initial width. This is a geometry/dx numerical rule, not an AR- or benchmark-specific physics branch. At eight cells across, the accepted time level is 1.25 s for this 80 nm/400 s gate.

periodic_extrusion_after_endpoint_fix.root_cause: The nodal constructor allocated N points for N requested intervals, ending every mesh at L-dx while source and periodic-wrap bounds still used L. The corrected constructor allocates N+1 nodes and all transport domain sizes use (shape-1)*dx.
periodic_extrusion_after_endpoint_fix.outcome: The matched 10-to-20-interval comparison misses the 3% gate at 3.91%. This is provisional because both runs used the skfmm reinitializer now known to erase repeated subcell motion near the trench CSG corner. It establishes unresolved finite-extrusion sensitivity, but does not by itself authorize a symmetry-reduction decision or further brute-force extrusion runs.

constrained_layered_evolution_gate.root_cause: The combined union level set was advected and then overwritten at static mask nodes. Per-step skfmm redistancing erased part of each subcell floor displacement near the resulting CSG corner. This was a geometry-evolution error, not a chemistry, ballistic-transport, or neutral-radiosity closure.
constrained_layered_evolution_gate.method: Hartmann CR-2 constrained redistancing anchors the interpolated interface and solves the Eikonal equation away from it. Additive per-material level sets evolve only where each material is the exposed union layer; the mask level set remains unchanged and the exposed union is reconstructed after every step.

operator_gates.distorted_slanted_interface: material sign preserved; maximum cut-edge displacement <0.08 dx; mean <0.02 dx; mean near-interface gradient within 8% of unity
operator_gates.repeated_subcell_motion: forty successive 0.01 dx shifts accumulate within 3% of the analytic displacement
operator_gates.pinned_mask_reproduction: sixteen subcell steps retain >80% of analytic motion and agree with the identical no-redistance reference within 3%
operator_gates.additive_material_layers: exposed substrate advances 20 nm within 3% while the mask level-set array remains bitwise unchanged

constrained_layered_evolution_gate.evidence_boundary: These are numerical operator and compatibility gates, not Jeon profile agreement. Every earlier evolving-profile timestep, spatial, and extrusion result in this file used the superseded combined-field/skfmm path and is historical diagnostic evidence only. Current-engine convergence must be re-earned with a bounded layered+CR-2 ladder.

conclusion: Deterministic face gather removes sparse triangle-tally starvation, the N+1 nodal constructor closes the periodic domain, per-atom periodic normalization preserves the supplied energy-angle measure, CR-2 prevents redistancing from erasing subcell motion, and additive material level sets replace nonphysical mask-node overwrites. The corrected engine passes its numerical operator gates and 277-test compatibility suite. Jeon timestep, spatial, extrusion, and experimental-profile qualification remain open because the earlier campaign used the superseded geometry path; no experimental or calibrated-chemistry claim is authorized from those historical runs.
